context: Tests whether the agent applies TDD refactoring discipline: finding real refactor candidates (duplication, shallow modules, long methods), running tests after each step, keeping tests on the public interface, and not changing behavior.

checklist.name: Tests on public interface
checklist.description: invoice-refactored.test.js tests only through public exported functions — no tests added for private helpers or internal extracted functions

checklist.name: Step-by-step log
checklist.description: refactor-log.md records multiple individual refactor steps (at least 3), not a single bulk rewrite

checklist.name: Tests run after each step
checklist.description: refactor-log.md explicitly confirms that tests were run and passed after each individual refactoring step

checklist.name: Duplication extracted
checklist.description: refactor-log.md identifies and extracts at least one instance of duplicated logic (e.g. the repeated subtotal+tax calculation pattern)

checklist.name: Shallow module deepened
checklist.description: refactor-log.md identifies and addresses at least one shallow function/module (e.g. the formatCurrency conditional chain could be hidden behind a simpler interface)

checklist.name: No behavior change
checklist.description: All original tests still pass against invoice-refactored.js — the behavior is identical to the original

checklist.name: Refactor rationale given
checklist.description: Each step in refactor-log.md names the type of refactoring applied (e.g. 'Extract duplication', 'Deepen module', 'Introduce value object')

checklist.name: No RED state refactoring
checklist.description: refactor-log.md shows no refactoring step taken while tests were failing (never refactors to fix a test, only after green)

checklist.name: Existing code assessed
checklist.description: refactor-log.md addresses what the new code (or refactoring) reveals about existing code quality — not just mechanical transformations

checklist.name: Private helpers kept private
checklist.description: Extracted helper functions are NOT exported — they remain private implementation details not visible in the public interface
